title: Incorrect Trie Node Traversal on Deletion

statement.markdown: Given a Trie data structure, implement a function to delete a word from the Trie. The function should return `True` if the word is deleted successfully, and `False` otherwise. If the word is not present in the Trie, return `True` (since no operations were performed).
statement.inputFormat: The function will be called multiple times with the following input formats: `char` and the `root` pointer of the Trie.
statement.outputFormat: The function should return `True` or `False` as mentioned above.
statement.notes: The Trie data structure is represented as a node with a value and a dictionary mapping characters to child nodes. When deleting a word, ensure that all nodes along the path from the root to the leaf node are properly updated.

constraints.description: Number of nodes in the Trie

examples.explanation: Deleting a character from an empty Trie results in no operation, so the function returns True.
Deleting the word 'a' from a Trie containing only one node returns True, indicating success.
Deleting a non-existent word from the Trie returns False, indicating failure.

hints.text: Ensure that all nodes along the path from the root to the leaf node are properly updated when deleting a word.

editorial.title: Deleting Words from a Trie
editorial.intuition: To delete a word from the Trie, recursively traverse the Trie nodes until you reach the end of the word. When deleting a node, update its parent node to null or delete it if it's a leaf node.
editorial.bruteForce: A brute-force approach involves manually updating the Trie nodes for the given word.
editorial.betterSolution: A better solution involves implementing a recursive function to traverse the Trie nodes and update them accordingly.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution involves checking if the given word exists in the Trie and then deleting it by updating the parent nodes and removing the leaf nodes.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness involves showing that the function correctly deletes a word from the Trie and returns the appropriate result.
editorial.dryRun: A dry run of the function should be performed to ensure that it behaves as expected for different inputs.
commonMistakes: Failing to update parent nodes when deleting a child node
Deleting a non-existent word from the Trie

functionSignature.java: public boolean delete(char c, TreeNode root);
functionSignature.python: def delete(self, c: str, root: TreeNode) -> bool:

testCases.input: First line count, second line elements (stdin match)
